subject: Re: Incorrect dose weighting and how to fix post-M
body:
Hi both,

I did a bit of digging and can confirm that the issue lies with the 
date/time within the mdoc file. An older dataset (with no issues) lists the 
dates as YY-Month-DD whereas my broken one is DD-Month-YYYY - I replaced 
all the instances across the mdoc files and they seem to be working now.
I think rather than rerunning the M alignments I will just take my updated 
dose weighting from the new xml files and overwrite the incorrect one in 
the old xml files so I can keep my M alignments just in case!
Thanks for all your help!

Cheers,

Mat

On Thursday, 8 June 2023 at 15:45:53 UTC+1 wim...@gmail.com wrote:

> The Tomo 5 mdoc file is wrong by writing only camera dose per image, not 
> sample dose prior and current (like SerialEM defines it). If there is a 
> possibility to enter dose manually, try that.
> Best,
> Wim
>
> Sent from my iPhone
>
> On 8. Jun 2023, at 15:05, cla...@gmail.com <cla...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿
>
> Hi all,
>
> I have been working with a tomographic dataset collected with the standard 
> dose-symmetric setup. The tilt series collection information has been found 
> in Warp from the mdoc files provided by Tomo 5 and I've done the 
> preprocessing, picking, extraction, refinement, etc. as normal. I'm now 
> reaching the resolution where M is being used to improve the resolution 
> further, refining the tilt angles and the volume warp grid. 
> Unfortunately I've now noticed that the dose hasn't been correctly ordered 
> by Warp so I now have incorrect dose weighting that will likely affect the 
> refinements at higher resolution. I checked the mdoc file (example 
> attached) and the collection order is definitely right so I'm assuming that 
> Warp is using it correctly for ordering by tilt angle but doing something 
> different for cumulative dose.
>
> As I have run alignments in M, I would like to keep these but update my 
> dose values and refine again, which I assume will improve the resolution 
> further. Has anyone come across this issue before who may know a way to 
> conveniently update the dose? I assume the tomogram xml and tomostar files 
> will need to be manually modified but this will take a very long time 
> especially when factoring in tilts that have been excluded! I've attached 
> an example mdoc file in case anyone can see issues with it.
>
> Cheers,
>
> Mat
>
> -- 
>

> .
> <Position_001.mdoc>
>
>
